metadata.title:
A Weyerhaeuser Magnate


in the Senate?

pages.raw_text:
La Toilette Robert Laird Mccormick the multi millionaire
of the lumber trade secretary of the Weyerhaeuser Tim
Der company the greatest timber trust earth and stand
pat politician of the old school, is trying to buy seat in
the United States senate from the great and growing tim
Der state of Washington.


Just when sentiment in the Middle West was reactng
against the corporation policies of Aldrch and Elkins
and Foraker Mccormick plehoric in wealth and Jealous
in politics of the brand that makes its motto get
thing, became possessed of nordnate desire to sit
in the United States senate


But things did not look hopeful in Wisconsin. La Fol
lee had scattered seeds of revolt against the dctaon OF
vested interests and privileged wealth, and Mccormick
saw the hopeessness of the timber trust and the timber
grabbers ever being represented from Wisconsin.


But out in the Far West senate seats selling the
market if not cheap at least they selling to the high
est bidder and with the of Clark and Ankeny be-
fore him and open market Mccormick rushed to Wash
ingtOn.


It an open secret that he for the specific
purpose of going the senate To boost him into the
limelight he nominated at so much per vote as
candidate for mayor of Tacoma in which city he had
settled to make his home.


But in Wisconsin, in Tacoma; the people were turn
ing from the theory that none but corporation friends may
be entrusted with power His known subservency to the
Northern Pacific railway his undong, and he went
down to defeat.


That four years ago And all these four years have
been spent reganng the prestige lost that municipal
conflict By of local and state phianthropies by
becoming the head of the State Historical society prom
nent in the Ferry museum and other public institutions
of quaseducatonal charitable nature he gradually
worked back up the ladder of political prestige into the
national commteemanshp of the party in the state That
last year This year he is busy signing endorsements
for federal appointments, and next fall the people will
lect senator.


The interests have decreed the retirement of Senator
Samuel H. Piles fearing that his record in their behalf will
him to be defeated He has announced that he will
not


And Mccormick has announced that he will run.


Further the politicians say he will not only run, but


he will pay The Weyerhaeuser barrel is back of him.


A lifetime ambition is urging him on and Robert
